name: Gregory W. Robertson
title: Undergraduate Student at UCLA
affiliation: College of Letters and Science, Department of Economics
bio: Hello! I'm Greg, an undergraduate student at UCLA currently pursuing a B.A. in Business Economics. I am broadly interested in studying behavioral economics, the intersection of technology and society, and the widespread and diverse impacts of AI.

My past research experiences include working with social media data, conducting literature reviews, and developing computational skills. This includes collecting and annotating data, writing code in Python, and preparing papers in LaTeX.

In my free time, I enjoy hitting the links (I was the captain of my high school golf team), learning new songs on the piano (I'm a big fan of classical pieces, like Chopin), and practicing Mandarin Chinese (which I earned a Seal of Biliteracy in). I also enjoy volunteering and have tutored math students, provided remote English learning lessons, and led a 20-student cultural exchange visit to Keelung, Taiwan.
